incorrects.description: Student reversed the numbers: placed 4 × 2 instead of 2 × 4

params.description: Bags highlight to draw attention to group count.

steps.text: You switched the numbers. Count the bags first. That's your groups number. Then count inside one bag. That's your in-each number.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect values on first attempt, not the reversed case

steps.text: Let's look closely. How many bags do you see?

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect values on second attempt, not the reversed case

params.description: Bags highlight one by one as system counts.

steps.text: Count the bags: 1, 2. That's 2 groups. Now pick one bag and count what's inside. How many items?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Bags highlight one by one. Then items in one bag highlight.
System places 2 tile in first slot, then 4 tile in second slot.
System places 4 tile in second slot.

steps.text: Here's how to build it. First I count the bags. 1, 2. Two bags means 2 groups, so I place 2 in the first slot. Then I look inside one bag and count the items. 1, 2, 3, 4. Four items means 4 in each group, so I place 4 in the second slot. The answer is 2 times 4, because that's what the picture shows: 2 groups of 4.
